text: Busy couples keep money, health records, household plans, and family responsibilities in different places.
Mithra brings those facts together.
Then it turns them into a calm weekly plan.
The Family Brief gives the household one shared view.
Finance tracks totals and trends. Health keeps reports and measurements together, without medical advice.
Planning holds dates and preparation. Each adult can keep selected records only for themselves.
Week in Review starts with a shared status: mostly on track.
It ranks travel documents, insurance renewal, and August household planning from valid shared records.
The insurance reminder and payment due date form one real-world event.
Mithra groups only strong matches, so the event appears once.
Groceries have used 14,500 of the recorded 18,000 budget, leaving 3,500.
Total spending is lower than the same point last month.
The result is a bounded next step, not an invented recommendation.
Progress shows a real change. The compact timeline keeps the next seven days ahead of later dates.
Empty sections stay hidden, so the review stays short.
Mithra keeps private information private.
The owner can see a glucose comparison and one unit-correction notice.
It names the measurement, preserves the recorded units, and makes no medical claim.
The partner view does not change shared status, summary, or empty states.
Each adult sees only their private information; household facts remain shared.
Codex inspected Mithra’s Go and SQLite app, then added Week-specific finance, planning, and health handling.
Codex added tests, privacy checks, browser verification, and this Remotion demo.
GPT-5.6 supported product critique, UX choices, prioritisation, prompt design, and review.
The builder made each scope, privacy, safety, and product decision.
Mithra calculates facts from evidence. Optional AI can interpret them, never invent or change records.
Mithra helps busy couples see what changed, prepare for what is next, and keep private facts private.
The hosted demo and public repository are ready to explore.
